introSection: Abyssus is a steel launched roller coaster at Energylandia in Zator, Małopolskie, Poland. It opened on 14 July 2021 as part of the new Aqualantis expansion.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Abyssus was scheduled to open in 2020, but the coronavirus pandemic caused the opening to be delayed to 2021.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Abyssus is a Shockwave 1320m installation from Vekoma. It has two launches as opposed to only one on the smaller Shockwave product. The ride is part of a new-for-2021 expansion called Aqualantis at Energylandia.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 3 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 16 riders per train.

fullText:
Abyssus is a steel launched roller coaster at Energylandia in Zator, Małopolskie, Poland. It opened on 14 July 2021 as part of the new Aqualantis expansion.

Abyssus was scheduled to open in 2020, but the coronavirus pandemic caused the opening to be delayed to 2021.

Abyssus is a Shockwave 1320m installation from Vekoma. It has two launches as opposed to only one on the smaller Shockwave product. The ride is part of a new-for-2021 expansion called Aqualantis at Energylandia.

3 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 16 riders per train.
